semantics: Edge (a, type, b) reads 'a is located_in b' (a is a transitive descendant / sub-region of b). 'contains' is the converse: (a, contains, b) reads 'a transitively contains b'. Composition: A located_in B, B located_in C => A located_in C; A contains B, B contains C => A contains C. NO other composition is defined.
note: DEGENERATE single-relation TRANSITIVE composition table. located_in is a strict partial order (antisymmetric, transitive) over place / administrative entities; 'contains' is its converse. The ONLY defined compositions are located_in o located_in = located_in and contains o contains = contains. located_in o contains and contains o located_in are UNDEFINED (they add nothing => SOUND: sibling/cousin places derive EMPTY). This is NOT a relation algebra (no general intersection/converse closure, no disjunctive relations, no point/interval algebra). It is the geographic/administrative-containment structural twin of the CLUTRR kinship composition table, parameterizing the SAME forward least-fixpoint UNION engine (kinship.py) unchanged. Emitted verbatim once in metadata.composition_table.

source.relation_primitive: Wikidata administrative-containment properties as annotated in DocRED/Re-DocRED (P131 'located in the administrative territorial entity', P17 'country', P150 'contains administrative territorial entity', P1376 'capital of', P36 'capital'), collapsed to the single primitive located_in (+ its converse contains).
source.engine: Reuses kinship.py (CLUTRR finite-composition forward least-fixpoint UNION closure) VERBATIM; the engine is parameterized entirely by this table.
source.scope: administrative / geographic containment only; antisymmetric transitive partial order.
